mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously whenever rate trajectories are downward-sloping.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.17% as of July 2016 indicates near-zero rates consistent with post-crisis accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.8% as of July 2016 indicates tight labor market conditions
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 5-day returns in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with accelerating housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity that drives corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both demand signals to strengthen jointly.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 381565M and HOUST at 529K as of June 2016
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns when both RSAFS and HOUST are rising are not significantly higher than when only one or neither is rising (ANOVA p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production over a 2-month window signals weakening economic capacity utilization and supply-side contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate margin compression.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment growth combined with declining unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that increases wage pressures and corporate labor costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as margin expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130265K and UNRATE at 4.8% as of July 2016
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage inflation risk
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when both PAYEMS growth accelerates and UNRATE declines are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2% annualized rate, it signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13382.6B (2017 dollars) as of Q2 2016
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of sub-2% GDP growth are not significantly lower than periods of >= 2% growth (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rates signals monetary tightening that increases real discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both inflation and rates to be elevated simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 220.19 and FEDFUNDS at 0.17% as of June 2016
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when both CPIAUCSL is rising and FEDFUNDS > 1.5% are not significantly lower than when either condition is absent (ANOVA p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) over a 2-month window signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate cyclical slowdown.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 529K units as of June 2016
regime_hint: housing market deterioration or demand-side weakness
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 5-day returns in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
